rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/9/2021. 1) The amounts under credits on GC-400 do not balance with the charges. It appears this needs to be amended. 2) Tolls are absorbed as part of fees and reimbursement is not allowed. (See LRSF 14.58 B.) Please explain why $8.20 was reimbursed to Thomas A. Lucas for a bridge toll. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/25/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: continued to 11/9/21 for the filing of a new declaration and attachment. a 13502 election must be made by the surviving spouse for her share of the CP. it is not about the decedent's share. filing deadline is noon on 11/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHOSTAC LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Request for trustee fees a. Trustee requests $60/hr. Non-professional fiduciaries are generally granted up to $45/hr. Trustee requests fees for petitioning to be appointed conservator over the person. Petitioner requests fees for what appears to be non-trustee services. b. 32 hours were spent in response to Court inquiry about actions she took, compiling records of invoices and time spent to care for Eugene and Eda which were paid by the trust. 2) Request for attorney fees a. Attorney already spent 62 hours (per 10/27/20 declaration supporting 1st account, totaling $23,906) to prepare the first accounting petition (which excluded the actual accounting that was prepared by a third party), for which attorney was already compensated for. Attorney now alleges that he spent an additional 19.25 hours on that same accounting petition (invoice dated 11/5/20) and requests an additional $7,343 for those services. Attorney invoice dated 1/4/21 also included work related to the first accounting, including to respond to Examiner's Notes (~$7k, based on combined rate). b. Other questionable entries: i. Charges for communicating with the bank, the accounting preparer and what looks to be the bookkeeper, which appears should have been performed by the trustee. ii. Inputting trust expenses into a spreadsheet. iii. Interoffice communications regarding update of status. iv. One hour to draft a letter to the probate department re: courtesy copies. v. Three total hours travel time to FedEx and Post Office. vi. Determining who is authorized to provide burial instructions, which does not appear to be the role of the trustee's attorney. 3) Court to determine whether court supervision should be terminated. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets Pursuant To Ca Probate Code 850; Compel Accounting From Prestina Trias From February 2019 To Present; For Damages; Disinheritance
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The proof of service of the amended petition does not show notice was given to the court-appointed attorney for the conservatee. The proof of service of the amended petition does not show notice was given to the attorney for respondents Prestina Trias (Macias) and Carlos Macias. The proof of service of the amended petition does not show Prestina Trias (Macias), Carlos Macias, and Lazaro Trias Jr. have been served in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a)(2) as persons claiming an interest in, or having title to or possession of the subject property. 2) The amended 850 petition only requests the Court's approval of the settlement agreement. The settlement agreement requires a new petition for an order voiding the deed and reverting title to the subject property back to the conservatee, concurrently with a petition for substituted judgment to transfer the property to the conservatee's existing trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Counsel is directed to explain the miscellaneous early release of funds on the Seller's Statement and to cite legal authority/cite probate code sections that authorize the early release of funds. Counsel should be prepared at the hearing to cite specific probate code sections. 2) Counsel is directed to file an accurate I&A that lists the Decedent's assets as of date of death. 3) Counsel is directed to file proof showing the assets are in an insured account and do not exceed the $250,000.00 FDIC limit. Examiner sent notes regarding the estate keeping assets over $250,000.00 in a single account. In the 08/19/2021 Amended Argument Why FDIC Insurance Is Not Required Petitioners cites unpublished cases and "California Rules of Court 81115(b)(1)." 4) Bank statement filed 7/19/2021 do not verify the balance at the end of the account period on 03/05/2021. [See LRSF 14.35 D.] Bank Letter dated 07/12/2021 alleges the current balance is $932,735.25. 06/30/2021 Bank Statement alleges balance on 04/01/2021 to 06/30/2021. 5) John Pollak was reimbursed $65.00 for parking. [See LRSF 14.58 B. Absorbed as part of fees.] 6) John Pollak was reimbursed $320.00 for E-filing costs Lightning Legal. No receipts provided. Usual court filing fee is $5.00/$10.00 per filing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE MARGARET STEINKELLNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter set for hearing for the court to determine whether to take the petition off calendar for repeated failures to respond to Examiner notes. How is San Francisco the proper venue when Decedent died in Milpitas and the real property is in Milpitas. a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)] The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. b. Must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). c. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] d. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Article Fifth of the decedent's will provides that: "I give, devise and bequeath my entire estate unto my beloved wife, MARGARET FINERMAN, provided however that should she not survive distribution to her, then and in that event my estate shall be divided into two (2) equal portions, one portion for the benefit of each of my sons, JEFFREY D. FIINERMAN and MICHAEL E. FINERMAN, and the surviving one thereof." Did the decedent's spouse, who died in February 2020, survive distribution of the decedent's interest in the Peabody property which was not held in joint tenancy? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICE MARY WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/9/2021. 1) The following person mentioned in Decedent's Will is not listed on page 4 under #8: Bryant Wong, [See petition page 3 #8 "All persons mentioned in decedent's will whether living or deceased"] The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D.] 2) A new Duties and Liabilities must be filed and include all the required information including the personal representatives telephone number. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/25/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARMEL ESTHER BALICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 10/18/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 11/01/2022.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GWYNNE R. MOFFITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/3/21. Examiner's Notes emailed on 10/7/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLARD D. YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 10/18/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 11/01/2022.
